title: branch — tenancy hooks
description: One tenancy hook: BranchesOnTenantErased. Distinct from `listeners.json` (which registers the same class as an event subscriber for compatibility with modules that dispatch via the event bus). Tenancy hooks fire at specific tenancy lifecycle points and participate in the tenancy transaction — see tenancy/hooks.json contract.

reference.hierarchy_note: See `hierarchy.md` §12 + `tenancy-columns.md` for the tenant plane the branch module lives on.

hooks.purpose: Runs BEFORE tenancy's FK cascade drops the tenant. Iterates every Branch row on the tenant + cancels any in-flight `GeocodeAddressJob` for those branches (via the queue's `cancel-by-tag` helper — the job carries a `branch:{id}` tag). Zero-cost when the tenant has no active geocoding jobs. Non-critical to the cascade — a failure here logs a warning + lets the cascade proceed (the subprocessor calls that leak through are billed to the platform, not the erased tenant).

notes: This module does NOT participate in tenancy's `Bootstrappers` (which run per-tenant setup like cache-prefix swap) because Branch is tenant-scoped data, not tenant-scoped runtime config.
The branch module intentionally has NO hook on `TenantProvisioned` — see readme §2. Branches are explicit tenant-admin onboarding actions, not auto-seeded on tenant creation.
See `.kiro/steering/hierarchy.md` §12 for the split between bootstrappers (per-request tenant switch) and hooks (lifecycle events).
